results.title: Paul Bocuse. Part 1
results.creator_credit: Didi von Rossek and Aida United
results.brief_description: These programs present Paul Bocuse’s favourite recipes for real food cooked at home in the simplest ways. From the first menu to the last, the six menus are all perfectly balanced; some simple and homely cuisine, others more elegant and elaborate. All the recipes are easy, with short, clear instructions that anyone can follow. Once you know how to prepare these meals you will be ready every occasion, especially as the recipes are accompanied by Paul Bocuse’s advice on how to vary the ingredients depending on the season, sensible shopping, etc. In this video he includes easy instructions, and printed guidelines for buying the necessary ingredients for both Veal Kidney Roast and Chocalte Mousse.

results.title: Simone de Beauvoir
results.brief_description: Simone De Beauvoir, Jean-Paul Satre and their friends discuss the events and involvements that shaped her life and opinions - from the Spanish Civil War to the Paris Riots of 1968. Her lucid, perceptive observations on people and events of the last 50 years exhibit the formidable power of exhilarating intelligence.

results.title: No myth
results.creator_credit: Claire Jager and Open Channel Productions
results.brief_description: This video exposes the entrenched attitudes and myths surrounding Australia’s most hidden crime: domestic violence. “No Myth” was produced to explain why secrecy, shame and misplaced guilt accompanies domestic violence, and why women who experience it are frequently too scared, ashamed and isolated to confide in anyone.

results.title: Insatiable
results.brief_description: A stylised, abstract musical narrative working around ideas of “performance”. Four singers who arrive at an old theatre for an audition. As they wait their turn to be called to perform, the singers gradually reveal through songs more about their pasts and speculate on their possible futures.

results.title: Louder than words
results.brief_description: A documentary about contemporary performance poetry featuring the work of Melbourne poets Ania Walwicz, Eric Beach and Jas H. Duke. They perform in their own exuberant, idiosyncratic and sometimes bizarre way a selection of their own works.

results.title: One people one soul
results.brief_description: The people of Irian Jaya are fighting a little known but bloody war of survival against the government of Indonesia. This program explores what is at stake in this war - a people and their country. The West Papuan people, as they prefer to call themselves, speak from guerilla hideouts in the jungle and from crowded refugee camps in Papua New Guinea.

results.title: Painting the Town
results.creator_credit: Trevor Graham, Ned Lander, Charles Merewether and Sharon Connolly
results.brief_description: This is a film about art and artists, culture and politics. Through the story of Yosl Bergner, the film reveals a community of Australian painters who sought to create an art that would reflect the great social upheavals of the 1930s and the 1940s. Yosl Bergner, Ruth Bergner, Jim Wigley and Albert Tucker discuss this period. Rated: G

results.title: In Between. Part 2
results.creator_credit: Chris Warner, Mandy Smith and Kim Dalton
results.brief_description: Two episodes of “In Between” a series of dramas set in Melbourne that looks at the pressures of growing up. Episode Three looks at Angie who is bright, tough and enjoys life. However, life is suddenly more complicated when she discovers that she is pregnant. Episode Four is about Alex who is confused by the different pressures that his father and friends place upon him to grow up.

results.title: All that glitters
results.brief_description: In September 1982, forty-five nations of the Commonwealth sent 1,500 athletes to Brisbane for the Twelfth Commonwealth Games. This program focuses on athletes and coaches, highlighting their relationships, lifestyles and training and their individual struggles. This film is the official film of the 12th Commonwealth Games, held in Brisbane, and is a combination of the films featured in the “Striving ” series.

results.title: The Steam-driven adventures of Riverboat Bill
results.brief_description: Based on the Riverboat Bill books by Cliff Green. Riverboat Bill and young Dave Freshwater restore an old paddle steamer, and, along with a fire-breathing Bunyip and Black Ben the Pirate, have many adventures sailing the Murray River.
